data.body:
PASS 1 review (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE → PASS.

This cluster is a byte-preserving DRY consolidation of canonical-JSON/content-hash helpers onto `_store/canonical.py`. It changes no persisted format and backfills no data: the plan explicitly preserves every site's exact output bytes (e.g. `ascii_only=True` to "PRESERVE `ensure_ascii=True`; do NOT normalize"), and AC #5 mandates a characterization test asserting migrated callers' output bytes are unchanged vs a frozen pre-refactor snapshot plus an unchanged sign→verify round-trip. Per the T11 overlay's anti-FP guidance (not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes), the migration-execution sub-checks (expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write window, rollback/data-loss) are vacuously satisfied — there is no migration to execute. No findings.
